heading: Purchase and sale of cable transfers, acceptances and bills
label: 353 Purchase and sale of cable transfers, acceptances and bills
text: Any Federal reserve bank may, under rules and regulations prescribed by the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System, purchase and sell in the open market, at home or abroad, either from or to domestic or foreign banks, firms, corporations, or individuals, cable transfers and bankers’ acceptances and bills of exchange of the kinds and maturities by this chapter made eligible for rediscount, with or without the indorsement of a member bank.
notes: References in TextThis chapter, referred to in text, was in the original “this Act”, meaning , , known as the Federal Reserve Act. CodificationSection is comprised of introductory provisions of section 14 of act . Subsecs. (a) to (g) of section 14 are set out as sections 354 to 359 and 348a of this title, respectively.Dec. 23, 1913
Change of NameSection 203(a) of act , changed name of Federal Reserve Board to Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System.Aug. 23, 1935
